titleStmt.title: Letter from John R. Cooper to Henry Arthur Wynne, 6 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John R. Cooper

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from John R. Cooper (1853-1918), crown solicitor for Wexford, to Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1943), the chief crown solicitor for Ireland. The letter is an application by Cooper for a months leave from his duties. Enclosed is a letter from his doctor recommending such action. A formal application is also attached, suggesting John R. Brennan for the position in Cooper's absence. A reply granting his leave is also attached.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5612/9630/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Custom House Quay,
Wexford , 6th June, 1916 . Dear Sir, I beg to submit Application for a
month's leave from my duties as Crown Solicitor
from the 12th June, 1916, owing to indisposition
and would like to hear from you at an early
date. I attach Doctor's Certificate Your faithfully, John R. Cooper The Chief Crown Solicitor,
DUBLIN CASTLE.
I certify that John R. Cooper Esq. Crown
Solicitor has been under my care and
I am of opinion that his attendances
at Court in the discharge of his
duties as crown solicitor would at
present be inadvisable, & I have
suggested that he should have
a months leave of absence
Sgt Furlong LR.C.P. & SI. June 5th 1916
I, JOHN R. COOPER, Crown Solicitor for the County of Wexford, beg
to apply for leave of absence from the 12th June 1916 to 12th July 1916.
Proposed Substitute : Qualification.
John R. Brennan. : Sixteen years practising Solicitor.
(Huggard & Brennan). :
Crown cases pending, or which the Crown Solicitor has been ordered to
take charge of, which may possibly come on during his absence.
Title of Cause: Nature of Case.
King - v - Patrick Fowler: Assault occasioning grievous bodily
: harm. (Quarter Sessions).
King - v - Martin Doyle : Larceny. (Quarter Sessions).
King - v - Patrick Leary : Robbery with Violence
: (Quarter Sessions).
King - v - William Coleman : Assault (Quarter Sessions).
Admiralty Commissioners : Fraudulent Attempt to obtain in—
- v- Julia Anne Sinnott : creased Separation Allowance.
: (Petty Sessions).
DATED this 6th day of June , 1916. Signed:- John R. Cooper
Crown Solicitor, WEXFORD .
8th June 1916
Leave of Absence Dear Sir,
The Attorney General approves of your having the leave
asked for. Yours truly, Henry Arthur Wynne JR Cooper Esq.,
Crown Solicitor,
Wexford.
